text:
Market today: Cautious optimism. Fed independence news (Cook ruling) removes one tail risk, and oil stabilizing above $70 is mild inflation noise. Hidden upside catalyst narrative for tech is the real driver today — watch whether rate expectations shift, because that's what moves your chip/tech names most.

Your holdings: AVGO (your largest, down 14.21%) is the priority — on margin, that hole is already painful and you're sitting above the $336 stop but not comfortably. PLTR (down 10.47%) is flagged SELL — no catalyst today to reverse it, and margin means you're paying to hold a loser. PTNM (down 15.25%) is a tiny spec but closest to its $9.50 stop percentage-wise — know your exit. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data; Charter/SpaceX news is positive color but don't size up on it.

Watch: 1) Any Fed rate-path commentary following the Cook ruling — directly impacts your tech multiples. 2) AVGO price action near $336 stop.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are on margin — tighten your plan on AVGO and PLTR before the market makes the decision for you.
